heroHeading: Modular Home Extensions, Built for Preston
metaTitle: Modular Home Extensions in Preston | EazyBase
metaDescription: Factory-built home extensions for Preston — designed for Fulwood, Penwortham and Longton homes, built in Blackburn and installed on site in under a week.
intro:
Preston is a city of family homes with room to breathe. From the tree-lined avenues of Fulwood to the newer estates around Penwortham and Longton, plots here tend to be that bit more generous — which makes them ideal candidates for a well-designed extension that adds real living space without eating the whole garden.

EazyBase builds modular home extensions in Preston a little differently. Your extension is designed around your home, then built in our Blackburn factory in as little as four weeks — warm, dry and quality-checked at every stage. When it arrives, installation on site typically takes under a week, so you're not living on a building site for months.

Whether it's a bigger kitchen-diner, a proper home office or a playroom the kids can actually spread out in, most of our projects fall between £20,000 and £60,000 — and every one starts with a conversation and a site visit.

localAngles.heading: Room to grow in Fulwood, Penwortham and Longton
localAngles.body: Preston's commuter belt is full of well-built family houses on plots that were laid out with a little generosity — rear gardens that can comfortably give up a few metres and still feel like gardens. That's the sweet spot for a single-storey modular extension. A factory-built kitchen-diner or garden room sits neatly on these plots, and because the structure arrives largely complete, your lawn and borders aren't churned up by months of trades coming and going.

localAngles.heading: Planning permission in Preston
localAngles.body: Many single-storey rear extensions fall within permitted development rights, meaning full planning permission often isn't needed — though limits on size, height and position always apply, so it's essential to check with Preston City Council (or South Ribble Borough Council if you're in Penwortham or Longton, south of the Ribble). We design every EazyBase extension with these rules in mind from day one, and we'll guide you through lawful development certificates or a planning application if your project needs one.

localAngles.heading: Why factory-built works so well here
localAngles.body: Our factory is in Blackburn, a short run from Preston along the M65 and M6. That proximity matters. Your extension is built indoors — no rain delays, no weather-damaged materials — then delivered and installed by a team who can be on your doorstep quickly for the survey, the installation and any follow-up. For homes on Preston's newer estates, with decent road access and driveway space, delivery day is usually refreshingly straightforward.

localAngles.heading: Space that earns its keep
localAngles.body: Preston homeowners tend to ask us for rooms that work hard: a kitchen-dining space where the family actually gathers, a home office for the days you're not on the train or the M6, or a playroom that keeps the toy explosion out of the lounge. Because each EazyBase extension is a proper insulated, plastered, heated room — not a conservatory — it's usable every day of a Lancashire year, from January onwards.

faqs.q: How long would an extension take for a home in Preston?
faqs.a: Your extension is built in our Blackburn factory in as little as four weeks while your garden stays untouched. Installation on site in Preston typically takes under a week. Being just down the M65, our team can visit for surveys and handovers without long lead times.

faqs.q: Will I need planning permission in Preston?
faqs.a: Often not — permitted development rights frequently cover single-storey rear extensions within set size and height limits. Always confirm with Preston City Council, or South Ribble Borough Council for Penwortham and Longton. We design to the rules and will handle the paperwork with you if an application is needed.

faqs.q: Can you deliver to my street? What about access and parking?
faqs.a: In most cases, yes. Modules arrive by lorry and are lifted into place, and the wider roads and driveways on much of Preston's housing stock make this straightforward. We assess access, parking and any lifting requirements during your free site survey, so there are no surprises on delivery day.

faqs.q: What do Preston homeowners typically use a modular extension for?
faqs.a: Kitchen-diners are the most popular, followed by home offices — a natural fit for Preston's commuters — plus kids' playrooms, dining rooms and garden rooms. All are fully insulated, heated rooms designed for year-round use, not fair-weather add-ons.
